sections.title: A: Multiple Choice (History of Writing)
sections.instructions: Choose the correct answer (A, B, C, or D).

sections.title: B: Multiple Choice (Vocabulary)
sections.instructions: Choose the correct answer (A, B, C, or D).

items.sentence: I was disappointed that the restaurant had ...... flowers on the table.
Sarah and Michael's ...... seems to make both of them unhappy.
Anybody found stealing from this shop will be .......
Grace thinks she's very ......, but I don't think many people like her, really.
I glanced at the newspaper and saw that the ...... said 'President Resigns'.
If our flight is delayed, will we ...... our connection in Los Angeles?
The doctor told Bill that he needed ...... on his arm.
The judge looked at the ...... and reminded him that he had to tell the whole truth.
I think my favourite ...... is probably table tennis.
The actor, Michael Read, was ...... today with robbery.

sections.title: C: Open Cloze (Your first telescope)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: 26 Many people look up at ...... night sky...
27 ...... that sounds like you, then perhaps it's time...
28 Astronomy ...... be a great hobby...
29 ...for those of you thinking of ...... it up.
30 You can still see a lot, as ...... as you don't expect...
31 ...to see so ...... detail.
32 You might ...... discover a comet!
33 Remember, though, that you ...... never look at the Sun directly...
34 ...you need to choose ...... a refracting and a reflecting telescope.
35 A refracting telescope is ...... great way of quickly...
36 ...quickly ...... your way around the heavens.
37 As well as more stars ...... you can imagine...
38 You should have no difficulty ...... finding Mars...
39 ...then you ...... need a reflecting telescope...
40 These telescopes are much ...... at collecting light...

sections.title: D: Multiple Choice (Grammar)
sections.instructions: Choose the correct answer (A, B, C, or D).

items.sentence: I think the discussion has gone on ...... and we should make a decision.
'Did you call John?' 'No, because I ...... go out. I'll try him later today.'
There ...... be a lot more open space around here before they built the new underground station.
If you hadn't lost the pieces, we ...... a game of chess.
'Why are you taking your PlayStation games?' 'Oh, just ...... Tommy wants to borrow them.'
I suppose we really ...... to book our ferry tickets in advance.
I'm afraid we've run out of ...... this week...
The man might have got away with the crime if the policeman ...... him.
I seem to be ...... busy to find the time for reading these days.'
The hotel has a poor reputation and ...... people want to stay there.

sections.title: E: Matching (Phrasal Verbs)
sections.instructions: Match to make sentences. There is one extra letter you will not use.

items.sentence: 51 The Pattersons have decided to pull
52 Mr Greene seems to find it difficult to get
53 We haven't made a final decision, but we've narrowed it
54 Dad thought it might be a good idea to look
55 It seems that Matt has fallen
56 I think it was Bob who came
57 The holiday started okay and then turned

sections.title: F: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
58 I lost my keys once before this month. **second**
This is the ...... my keys this month.

59 I started playing squash six years ago. **for**
I ...... six years.

60 I can't wait until I'm old enough to go to a match on my own. **forward**
I'm really ...... old enough to go to a match on my own.

61 Accidents are often caused by careless driving. **results**
Careless driving ...... accidents.

62 It's a waste of time denying that you did it when we've got proof. **point**
There ...... that you did it when we've got proof.

63 Don't you wish you could travel into space? **able**
Wouldn't you love ...... into space?

64 I called the travel agent to check that I had the right timetable. **make**
I called the travel agent to ...... I had the right timetable.

65 I'm afraid you're too young to go down the water slide. **old**
I'm afraid you ...... to go down the water slide.

66 My mum says doctors weren't so expensive in the past. **used**
My mum says doctors ...... so expensive.

sections.title: G: Error Correction (Extra word)
sections.instructions: If a line is correct, put a tick (✓). If there is an extra word, write it.

items.sentence: 67 I had a great week at school! We were been having a media
68 studies lesson when our teacher told to us that we were going
69 to make a radio advert for the school! I was really keen on
70 to be involved because I've always had been interested in a
71 career in the television. She asked us to plan our advert in
72 detail and to write a script. Most people found it out difficult
73 to come up with ideas, but I didn't. I decided that I would have
74 interviews with people who were used to go to the school,
75 commenting on how they had benefited them from going to that
76 school. My teacher thought it was a great idea and said I should
77 to see if I could find some ex-students. I asked the head and
78 she gave me a few phone numbers. When I called them and
79 explained them what I wanted to do, they were all happy to
80 help. I am going to visit them with a tape recorder and record
81 that what they say. Media studies is definitely turning out to be my favourite subject!

sections.title: H: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Use the word in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap.

items.sentence: 82 Some ...... (CRIME) become known for their intelligence...
83 Others are just so stupid that it seems ...... (RIDICULE).
84 Take, for instance, one ...... (ROB), who decided that he was feeling a little tired...
85 Seeing the ...... (COMFORT) bed, he decided to take a nap.
86 It may seem ...... (BELIEF), but he was still asleep when the owners got home!
87 They ...... (NERVOUS) called the police...
88 Another ...... (HUMOUR) story is that of the man who stole a...
89 ...a ...... (SECURE) camera.
90 He managed to steal the camera ...... (EQUIP), but left the tape behind.
91 It was used as ...... (EVIDENT) in court because...
